feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: 2 researchers charged with smuggling mpox into the US
entry.summary: Employed at a National Institutes of Health lab in Montana, the two allegedly brought deactivated virus from the Republic of the Congo without a permit.

entry.title: Five people have left hantavirus quarantine facility in Nebraska
entry.summary: Some passengers from a hantavirus-striken cruise ship have returned to their home states, where they are under the watch of public health authorities for the remainder of their monitoring period.

entry.title: It’s a fight for two lives: a mother is trying to survive for her unborn daughter
entry.summary: IDAHO FALLS — A local husband and his family are facing a situation they never imagined. He is fighting for his wife’s life while also trying to protect their unborn baby. They additionally have to relocate to another state on short notice for necessary care …

entry.title: Woman Says Doctor Told Her to Cancel a Scan. Then She Was Diagnosed with Stage 4 Cancer
entry.summary: "If something doesn’t feel right with your health, it probably isn’t," said Clare Din

entry.title: 5 Best Anti-Inflammatory Carbs You Should Be Eating, According to a Dietitian
entry.summary: You may be surprised to hear that carbs can be anti-inflammatory. Learn what 5 of the best anti-inflammatory carbs are that dietitians want you to eat.

entry.title: Why Google wants to release millions of mosquitoes in the US
entry.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

entry.title: Number of suspected Ebola cases falls by hundreds as testing ramps up
entry.summary: The number of cases falls from 1,100 to 437 with increased testing.

entry.title: Even One Drink a Day Could Raise Your Cancer Risk, Study Warns
entry.summary: For some health conditions like cancer, there's no safe level of alcohol consumption, a large new review finds.

entry.title: 4 Standing Exercises That Shrink Belly Overhang Faster Than Gym Workouts After 55
entry.summary: A CPT shares 4 standing exercises that strengthen the core and address belly overhang after 55.

entry.title: Can marriage improve your health? Here are the benefits that experts see.
entry.summary: Saying “I do” may influence your cancer risk, mental health and more.

entry.title: Google wants to set 64 million mosquitoes loose in California and Florida
entry.summary: The company’s ‘Debugging’ division hopes to reduce insect-borne illnesses by releasing millions of sterile males into the wild

entry.title: Get to know your TRICARE health plan with the TRICARE Plans Overview Fact Sheet
entry.summary: Learn about your TRICARE health plan today with a simple tool that makes it easy. The TRICARE Plans Overview Fact Sheet is your go-to resource for understanding what you and your family members can

entry.title: 5 Chair Exercises That Restore Walking Strength Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: A CPT shares 5 chair moves that restore walking strength after 60.

entry.title: Goodbye CPAP? New Pill Shows Major Promise for Sleep Apnea
entry.summary: A large phase 3 clinical trial has revealed promising results for a once-nightly pill designed to treat obstructive sleep apnea by targeting the biological mechanisms that cause airway collapse during sleep. A once-nightly pill significantly improved obstruct…

entry.title: Ranking high blood pressure drug combinations from most to least tolerated
entry.summary: The Global Hypertension Report by the World Health Organization (WHO) revealed that 1.4 billion people were living with hypertension in 2024. Yet, only a little over one in five of those diagnosed have it under control, whether through medication or by managi…

entry.title: Biohybrid microrobots repair spinal cord by combining stem cells with magnetoelectric nanoparticles
entry.summary: Spinal cord injuries can have devastating consequences for those affected. Nerve cells in the spinal cord rarely regenerate naturally, while scarring often prevents the regrowth of nerve fibers. Modern therapies attempt to influence implanted stem cells using…

entry.title: How Ebola kills — and what it takes to stop it
entry.summary: It's a virus that can strike with unrelenting force. The kind of care need to knock it out is often not fully available in a lower resource country like the Democratic Republic of Congo.

entry.title: The Worst Breakfast Food for Your Cholesterol (Hint: It’s Not Eggs)
entry.summary: Registered dietitians share the worst breakfast food for your cholesterol, plus healthier breakfast alternatives.

entry.title: Why Dallas-Fort Worth Bartenders Are Seeing More Zebra Striping This Year
entry.summary: A growing number of North Texans are embracing a drinking trend known as “zebra striping,” alternating alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages.

entry.title: Researchers may have discovered the key to understanding human consciousness
entry.summary: Scientists discover a hidden thalamic brain rhythm that appears during wakefulness and REM sleep.

entry.title: Mom Thought She Just Had Slipped Disc, Scan Reveals Horrifying Reality
entry.summary: Rachel Smith told Newsweek that the back pain left her “unable to walk," but she never could have guessed what scans would show.

entry.title: Google applies for permit to release 32 million mosquitoes in Calif.
entry.summary: Google's Debug Project wants to use sterile mosquitoes to stop the deadly bug from reproducing.

entry.title: Men who looksmaxx are also brutally fertility-mogging themselves
entry.summary: Men who looksmaxx are also brutally fertility-mogging themselves.

entry.title: This scientist learned he has a devastating brain disease. He set out to cure it.
entry.summary: Jeff Carroll is part of a new effort to accelerate the development of targeted cures for a handful of neurodegenerative diseases.

entry.title: Scientists discover how coffee interacts with the gut microbiome to affect the human brain
entry.summary: Regularly drinking coffee modifies your gut microbiome, which in turn influences mood, stress, and memory. New research reveals that both caffeinated and decaf coffee offer distinct, measurable benefits for psychological well-being and digestive health.

entry.title: Westmoreland County overdose deaths drop to lowest level in more than a decade
entry.summary: Drug overdose deaths in West­moreland County plummeted last year to their lowest level in more than a decade, according to a report from Coroner John Ackerman. The data revealed 72 people died from drug overdoses in the county last year, continuing a fo…

entry.title: My Boyfriend Left Me for Dead in His Apartment. I’m Not Sure I Can Ever Look at Him the Same Way.
entry.summary: This is unforgivable.

entry.title: 2026 Ebola outbreak: Maps track spread and global impact data of virus
entry.summary: The rapid spread of Africa’s Ebola outbreak is driven by a new strain known as Bundibugyo. Maps and charts detail its spread.

entry.title: Why is Michigan loosening its rules for parents wanting to exempt kids from vaccines?
entry.summary: A decade ago, Michigan had high rates of parents not vaccinating their children, so it required them to attend an in-person education class to get an exemption. It worked — until things got ugly.

entry.title: Conservatives and Liberals Have Diverging Health Outcomes. Why?
entry.summary: A new study examines an emerging health gap between Americans on the right and left.

entry.title: The Ketamine Revolution
entry.summary: Once known as a club drug, ketamine is helping the ‘walking wounded.’

entry.title: One fat helped pancreatic cancer grow while another cut disease in half
entry.summary: A surprising new study suggests that when it comes to pancreatic cancer, the kind of fat you eat may matter more than how much. Researchers found that oleic acid—the main fat in olive oil and several other common foods—sped up tumor growth in mice predisposed…

entry.title: In search of a facelift without a knife — the rise of stem cell tourism
entry.summary: Longevity clinics and medical spas are increasingly offering their own — often unsubstantiated — treatments. But do they work?

entry.title: One Common Food Can Fuel Your Workouts And Aid Recovery. Here's The Science.
entry.summary: Honey has been used by humans as a natural sweetener and energy source to sustain work and physical performance for thousands of years.

entry.title: Some hantavirus-exposed cruise ship passengers return home to finish quarantine
entry.summary: Several Americans who were exposed to a rare strain of hantavirus on board a cruise ship this spring have returned home to finish the second half of their mandatory six-week quarantine.

entry.title: 5 American cruise ship passengers leave Nebraska quarantine facility
entry.summary: Five American cruise ship passengers exposed to hantavirus are leaving a Nebraska quarantine facility. U.S. health officials say they will complete monitoring at home after remaining symptom-free. They arrived in Nebraska three weeks ago following a deadly ha…

entry.title: Basil and fennel compound may build up dangerous DNA damage in vulnerable people
entry.summary: Methyleugenol is a component of essential oils and is found in basil, tarragon, nutmeg, and fennel. When ingested through food, it can be converted in the liver into a reactive form that induces chemical alterations in DNA. "These so-called methyleugenol-deri…

entry.title: The (heartbreaking) truth about what happens to seniors when family stops visiting
entry.summary: She says don’t worry, she stays busy, she has her shows. And because she sounds okay — because she doesn’t complain — you believe her. But the research tells...

entry.title: Mild winter means more ticks. Here's how to avoid them
entry.summary: Ticks are more than just a nuisance. They can carry diseases such as Lyme disease, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, tularemia and Colorado tick fever.

entry.title: DBS Remodels White Matter Paths to Reverse Depression
entry.summary: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) physically remodels white matter pathways to cure severe depression.

entry.title: Landmark cancer trial shows success against ‘undruggable’ cancer — raising hopes for future treatments
entry.summary: Unprecedented results against a stubbornly hard-to-treat cancer are boosting optimism that other challenging tumours will be next.

entry.title: Scabies outbreak reported at Muskegon Correctional Facility
entry.summary: Several housing units at the Muskegon Correctional Facility are under quarantine due to an outbreak of a highly contagious skin infection, officials confirmed to News 8.

entry.title: Two possible Ebola cases in Brazil ruled out as patients test negative
entry.summary: The two patients had recently returned from the DR Congo and Uganda respectively.

entry.title: ‘High’ levels of potentially deadly virus in Boise. How to keep kids safe
entry.summary: Rotavirus levels are high in Boise, Idaho. Learn symptoms, dehydration signs, prevention tips and when to seek medical care for children and adults.

entry.title: Here’s What Happens When You Drink Green Tea Every Day, According to Registered Dietitians
entry.summary: Discover what happens to your body when you drink green tea daily, according to registered dietitians. The popular drink is packed with antioxidants and more.

entry.title: Woman Dismissed as Perimenopausal Gets Shocking Diagnosis
entry.summary: Her symptoms seemed relatively harmless, but her true diagnosis was anything but.

entry.title: Urgent warning as deadly West Nile virus is discovered in California
entry.summary: The Coachella Valley Mosquito and Vector Control District announced that a mosquito sample collected in the North Shore community tested positive for West Nile virus.

entry.title: Dermatologists and Scientists Agree that These Are the Only Proven Ways to Build Collagen and Fight Wrinkles
entry.summary: Discover dermatologist-approved ways to build collagen in your skin and learn which collagen tips actually work for firmer, younger-looking skin.

entry.title: Outdated Boomer Hygiene Advice Millennials Absolutely Can't Stand
entry.summary: Per research and online opinions, millennials tend to be more mindful about food storage, handwashing, using bar soaps, and washing their hair than boomers.

entry.title: ‘Gold Standard’ for Mental Health Diagnosis May Leave Patients Miscategorized, Study Finds
entry.summary: A new meta-analysis of 57 psychological studies found that essentially identical patient interviews can lead to different diagnoses for the same exact patient.
